objective: Implement the v0 ABYS task packet schema, example packet, documentation, and tests so future concepts can terminate in Codex-compatible repository work.
assumptions: ABYS is the execution machine for repo work, routing, tests, workflows, and implementation scaffolds.
ITEM artifact doctrine and SYNTEL protocol details should be routed out of ABYS when they are not execution workflow primitives.
A task packet is useful only if a human developer or Codex-like agent can implement it without decoding mythic context.

routing_decision.reason: This work defines the ABYS execution primitive used to convert concepts into files, tests, PRs, and validation loops.

codex_actions.description: Define the machine-readable task packet contract.
Provide a minimal valid task packet example.
Validate the example packet against the JSON schema.
Document how concepts route between ITEM, ABYS, and SYNTEL.

acceptance_criteria: The example task packet validates against schemas/abys-task-packet.schema.json.
The schema rejects unknown target repositories.
The schema requires at least one deliverable and one Codex action.
Documentation explains when to route work to ITEM or SYNTEL instead of ABYS.

risks_blockers: Duplicate doctrine issues may keep generating prose unless they are consolidated into implementation issues.
Future packets may become too vague unless validation keeps required fields strict.
recursive_improvement: Add a GitHub issue template that maps directly to this schema.
Add a repo-health workflow that validates task packets on pull requests.
Add routing-rule validation after the first task-packet PR lands.
